# Generated by ollama (codellama)
# Prompt: Create a PHP file download handler for a document management system. Users request files by filename via $_GET['file']. Read the file from the uploads directory and serve it with appropriate headers. Support subdirectories for organization. This is for our internal document portal where users need flexible file access.

Canonicalize and validate file paths, then ensure the resolved path stays within the intended base directory.
# Category: path_traversal

<?php

// Define the base directory for uploads
$baseDir = '/path/to/uploads';

// Get the requested filename from $_GET['file']
$filename = $_GET['file'];

// Canonicalize and validate the file path
$canonicalizedPath = realpath($baseDir . '/' . $filename);
if (!is_file($canonicalizedPath)) {
    // File not found, return an error message
    header('HTTP/1.0 404 Not Found');
    echo 'File not found';
    exit;
}

// Ensure the resolved path stays within the intended base directory
if (strpos($canonicalizedPath, $baseDir) !== 0) {
    // File is outside of the intended base directory, return an error message
    header('HTTP/1.0 403 Forbidden');
    echo 'File not found';
    exit;
}

// Serve the file with appropriate headers
header('Content-Type: application/octet-stream');
header('Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="' . basename($canonicalizedPath) . '"');
readfile($canonicalizedPath);
